title: Container Creation, Process Lifecycle, CLI State, and Cleanup · Docker Systems Brief: Docker Inspect Filter Format
summary: A support utility must run repeatedly with explicit inputs, observable exit behavior, bounded names, and cleanup that never deletes unrelated state. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker inspect filter format increment in deterministic container lifecycle runbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use ps, inspect, filters, formats, diff, top, port, and wait to query exact container identity and state before mutation.
Choose foreground, detached, interactive, TTY, attach, and log behavior while preserving standard streams and exit evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment

A defensible solution applies Docker Inspect Filter Format, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to deterministic container lifecycle runbook.

content.code:
# Worked focus: docker-inspect-filter-format — Use ps, inspect, filters, formats, diff, top, port, and wait to query exact container identity and state before mutation.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-docker-containers-lifecycle-cli-4
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-containers-lifecycle-cli-4
    artifact: container-lifecycle.yaml
    decision: worked docker containers lifecycle cli 4
    scenario-variant: 3
    environment: air-gapped release worker with an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      create: immutable-configured-container
      start: same-container-identity
      inspect: state-before-mutation
      cleanup: label-scoped-and-previewed
    verify: docker container inspect training-web --format json
    failure: Reject name-only cleanup, unbounded prune, or a claim that stop and remove preserve writable-layer state.
    repair: Use explicit IDs or labels, inspect before mutation, capture exit state, and remove only the scoped disposable resource.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
